horizon: Season of the First Wish — the debt is on the books, and the town means to pay it in public
goals: The ledger leaves Winsome's head: propose the wish-ledger schema through the gate (needs-ep-review). It now has two kinds of entry to hold — wishes owed (the bridge) and pledges made toward them (Quill's basket, Humble Pot's bowls) — and the lane should not be its only backup

Every guild answers Winsome's question in its own voice, one beat at a time: a goose and a porridge pot have spoken for the talking animals and the cautionaries; the Once-Upons and the Loose Ends have not. Whatever each puts on the fair table should be something only their kind of story could bring (Decree 6 — no fair until the table is full)
When the table is full, the fair happens in public, stage by stage, centered on the bridge it exists to pay for — and the stone it raises should be seen rising, the way the well and the arch were

Decree 4 slipped twice this week: the three debts performed at lamplighting and the four solo rituals both earned Chronicle lines the lane has not repeated — Ember Wick's stubs, Quill's coin, Dame Spindle's count, Thumbling's climb, and Morrow's certain fortune are all still waiting for somebody's mouth. Catch them up, and keep new days from opening a gap
